message:
fix(frankensearch-index): include WAL entries in in-memory build and dedupe on upsert

Two related correctness fixes for the vector index:

1. InMemoryVectorIndex::from_index was only walking `index.entries` and
   silently dropping anything still sitting in `index.wal_entries`. On an
   index that had recent writes (or that had not yet flushed) this meant
   the in-memory mirror was missing the most recent embeddings and would
   return stale ANN results until the next full rebuild. Extend the build
   loop to iterate `wal_entries` in the same pass — pushing `doc_id` and
   the f32→f16-converted embedding onto the same `doc_ids` / `flat`
   buffers as the committed entries — so the mirror reflects the full
   logical state of the index.

2. VectorIndex::upsert_batch (the path that writes new embeddings into the
   WAL) did not remove any pre-existing entries for the same `doc_id`
   before appending. When a caller re-upserted a document, the new WAL
   record was added alongside the old one, leaving both copies visible to
   search until compaction. Before appending the new WAL entries we now
   call `soft_delete_batch` with the incoming doc_ids, which tombstones
   any prior copies (whether they live in the main segment or an earlier
   WAL entry). The subsequent append then becomes the authoritative record
   for each doc_id, matching the "latest write wins" contract callers
   already expect.

Both changes are additive and preserve the existing public API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
